themes: Josh Allen all-time TD record chase
New coaching staff under Joe Brady
writeup: June 8, 2026 was all about anticipation in Buffalo, as the Bills stood on the eve of their three-day mandatory minicamp — the first under new head coach Joe Brady and defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard. Beat writers and analysts turned their attention to what to expect on the practice field, with storylines ranging from Josh Allen's path to becoming the NFL's all-time total touchdown record holder to how Leonhard's revamped defensive scheme would take shape. The day's coverage also spotlighted which players had the most to prove heading into the summer sessions, underscoring the sense of renewal and heightened expectations surrounding the post-McDermott era in Orchard Park. With the new Highmark Stadium looming on the horizon and Super Bowl ambitions very much alive, the Bills and their fanbase were buzzing with cautious optimism.
